20_26_1.NKJV: As snow in summer and rain in harvest, So honor is not fitting for a fool.

20_26_1.NASB: Like snow in summer and like rain in harvest, So honor is not fitting for a fool.

20_26_1.NRSVCE:

20_26_2.NKJV: Like a flitting sparrow, like a flying swallow, So a curse without cause shall not alight.

20_26_2.NRSVCE:

20_26_3.NKJV: A whip for the horse, A bridle for the donkey, And a rod for the fool’s back.
20_26_3.KJV: A whip<S>7752</S> for the horse<S>5483</S>, a bridle<S>4964</S> for the ass<S>2543</S>, and a rod<S>7626</S> for the fool's<S>3684</S> back<S>1460</S>.

20_26_4.NKJV: Do not answer a fool according to his folly, Lest you also be like him.

20_26_4.NRSVCE:

20_26_5.NKJV: Answer a fool according to his folly, Lest he be wise in his own eyes.
20_26_5.KJV: Answer<S>6030</S> a fool<S>3684</S> according to his folly<S>200</S>, lest he be wise<S>2450</S> in his own conceit<S>5869</S>. <sup>conceit: Heb. eyes</sup>

20_26_5.NASB: Answer a fool as his folly [deserves], That he not be wise in his own eyes.
20_26_5.NLT:

20_26_5.WEB: Answer a fool according to his folly, lest he be wise in his own eyes.
20_26_5.CSB17:

20_26_5.NRSVCE:

20_26_6.NKJV: He who sends a message by the hand of a fool Cuts off his own feet and drinks violence.
20_26_6.KJV: He that sendeth<S>7971</S> a message<S>1697</S> by the hand<S>3027</S> of a fool<S>3684</S> cutteth off<S>7096</S> the feet<S>7272</S>, and drinketh<S>8354</S> damage<S>2555</S>. <sup>damage: or, violence</sup>

20_26_6.NASB: He cuts off [his own] feet [and] drinks violence Who sends a message by the hand of a fool.
20_26_6.NLT:

20_26_6.WEB: One who sends a message by the hand of a fool is cutting off feet and drinking violence.

20_26_7.NKJV: Like the legs of the lame that hang limp Is a proverb in the mouth of fools.

20_26_7.NRSVCE:

20_26_8.NKJV: Like one who binds a stone in a sling Is he who gives honor to a fool.

20_26_8.NRSVCE:

20_26_9.NKJV: Like a thorn that goes into the hand of a drunkard Is a proverb in the mouth of fools.
20_26_9.KJV: As a thorn<S>2336</S> goeth up<S>5927</S> into the hand<S>3027</S> of a drunkard<S>7910</S>, so is a parable<S>4912</S> in the mouth<S>6310</S> of fools<S>3684</S>.

20_26_10.NKJV: The great God who formed everything Gives the fool his hire and the transgressor his wages.
20_26_10.KJV: The great<S>7227</S> God that formed<S>2342</S> all things both rewardeth<S>7936</S> the fool<S>3684</S>, and rewardeth<S>7936</S> transgressors<S>5674</S>. <sup>The great: or, A great man grieveth all, and he hireth the fool, he hireth also transgressors</sup>

20_26_10.NRSVCE:

20_26_11.NKJV: As a dog returns to his own vomit, So a fool repeats his folly.

20_26_11.NASB: Like a dog that returns to its vomit Is a fool who repeats his folly.

20_26_11.NIV:

20_26_11.NRSVCE:

20_26_12.NKJV: Do you see a man wise in his own eyes? There is more hope for a fool than for him.

20_26_12.NLT:

20_26_12.AMP:

20_26_13.NKJV: The lazy man says, “There is a lion in the road! A fierce lion is in the streets!”
20_26_13.KJV: The slothful<S>6102</S> man saith<S>559</S>, There is a lion<S>7826</S> in the way<S>1870</S>; a lion<S>738</S> is in the streets<S>7339</S>.

20_26_13.AMP:

20_26_13.MSG:
Loafers say, "It's dangerous out there!

20_26_13.NIV:

20_26_13.NRSVCE:

20_26_14.NKJV: As a door turns on its hinges, So does the lazy man on his bed.
20_26_14.KJV: As the door<S>1817</S> turneth<S>5437</S> upon his hinges<S>6735</S>, so doth the slothful<S>6102</S> upon his bed<S>4296</S>.

20_26_14.CSB17:

20_26_14.NIV:

20_26_14.NRSVCE:

so does a lazy person in bed.

20_26_15.NKJV: The lazy man buries his hand in the bowl; It wearies him to bring it back to his mouth.
20_26_15.KJV: The slothful<S>6102</S> hideth<S>2934</S> his hand<S>3027</S> in his bosom<S>6747</S>; it grieveth<S>3811</S> him to bring it again<S>7725</S> to his mouth<S>6310</S>. <sup>it grieveth: or, he is weary</sup>

20_26_15.WEB: The sluggard buries his hand in the dish. He is too lazy to bring it back to his mouth.

20_26_15.NIV:

20_26_15.NRSVCE:

20_26_16.NKJV: The lazy man is wiser in his own eyes Than seven men who can answer sensibly.
20_26_16.KJV: The sluggard<S>6102</S> is wiser<S>2450</S> in his own conceit<S>5869</S> than seven men<S>7651</S> that can render<S>7725</S> a reason<S>2940</S>.

20_26_16.NLT:

20_26_16.CSB17:

20_26_16.NRSVCE:

20_26_17.NKJV: He who passes by and meddles in a quarrel not his own Is like one who takes a dog by the ears.
20_26_17.KJV: He that passeth by<S>5674</S>, and meddleth<S>5674</S> with strife<S>7379</S> belonging not to him, is like one that taketh<S>2388</S> a dog<S>3611</S> by the ears<S>241</S>. <sup>meddleth: or, is enraged</sup>

20_26_17.NASB: [Like] one who takes a dog by the ears Is he who passes by [and] meddles with strife not belonging to him.

20_26_17.CSB17:

is like one who grabs a dog by the ears.

Is he who, passing by, stops to meddle with a dispute that is none of his business.
20_26_17.MSG:

20_26_17.NIV:

20_26_17.NRSVCE:
